_cap_override.author: human-review
_cap_override.note:
Demoted from Mandatory to Preferred per 2026-05-14 vendor evidence
review. Zero vendors market budgeting on pricing pages; only Xero
mentions cash-flow forecast (and that's at every tier including
Starter). SMEs typically do budgeting in spreadsheets, not in
their accounting tool. Not splitting into separate budget +
forecast caps — no commercial differentiation signal yet
(revisit if AI-grounded forecasting becomes a distinct premium
tier).

vendor_questions.question_terse:
Is your solution listed on IMDA InvoiceNow-Ready Solution Provider (IRSP) Listing?

[1] If you are the Product Principal of the solution, you are required to be accredited as an IRSP by IMDA. [2] If you are the reseller of the solution, your Solution Product Principal must declare to IMDA that you are an authorised reseller of their solution.
Note: For more information on InvoiceNow, refer to: Becoming an InvoiceNow-Ready Solution Provider | E-invoicing | IMDA (https://www.imda.gov.sg/how-we-can-help/nationwide-e-invoicing-framework/becoming-an-invoicenow-ready-solution-provider)
vendor_questions.question_t2: Listed on the IMDA InvoiceNow-Ready Solution Provider (IRSP) Listing

universal SG SME shape.

Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your finance system is accessible, secure, and connected to the rest of your business. Singapore SMEs must comply with PDPA data protection rules and need staff to access financial data safely from anywhere—especially in hybrid work environments. It matters when your accountant needs to approve an invoice from a client site, or when you're integrating bank feeds and tax filings with external authorities.

Core Ledger & Close.narrative: This is the financial backbone—it records every transaction in double-entry format, tracks fixed assets, and produces the profit-and-loss and balance-sheet statements your bank and tax authority require. Every Singapore SME must close its books monthly and file annual accounts with ACRA; this family automates the checklist and audit trail to save time and reduce errors. It matters when you're preparing for year-end audit or need to explain a variance to your bank.

Revenue Cycle.narrative: This family handles customer invoicing, payment collection, and credit management—the lifeblood of cash flow. Singapore SMEs benefit from e-invoicing via InvoiceNow (PEPPOL) to speed up payment, and from automated dunning to chase overdue invoices without manual follow-up. It matters when a customer delays payment and you need to know your exposure, or when you want to accept PayNow and credit-card payments online.

Expense Cycle.narrative: This family captures supplier bills, employee expenses, and purchase orders in one place, then routes them for approval and payment. SMEs save time by automating three-way matching (PO, receipt, invoice) and by letting staff submit expense claims via mobile instead of email. It matters when you need to control spending across multiple projects or departments, or when you want to reconcile supplier invoices without manual data entry.

Treasury & Compliance.narrative: This family manages cash, tax, and regulatory filing—critical for SME survival and compliance. Singapore SMEs must file GST returns (F5) on time, reconcile bank accounts, and forecast cash to avoid overdrafts; multi-currency support is vital if you trade across ASEAN. It matters when you're managing multiple bank accounts, preparing a GST return, or forecasting whether you'll have enough cash to pay suppliers next month.

Planning & Analytics.narrative: This family lets you build budgets, compare actual spending to plan, and spot trends via dashboards—turning raw transactions into business insight. SMEs use budgeting to control costs and forecasting to plan growth; AI-powered anomaly detection can flag unusual invoices or fraud early. It matters when you're planning next year's hiring, or when you want to see which customer or project is most profitable at a glance.
